(IPA: [[ko:ʈ:ajam]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Malayalam "Help:IPA/Malayalam")) is a [city](/wiki/City "City") in the [Kottayam district](/wiki/Kottayam_district "Kottayam district") of [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). It is the district headquarters of the district and is located about 151 km (93.8 mi) north of the state capital [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram"). As per the [2011 Indian census](/wiki/2011_Indian_census "2011 Indian census"), Kottayam has a population of 136,812 people, and a population density of 2,470/km2 (6,400/sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

**Kottayam** (IPA: [[ko:ʈ:ajam]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Malayalam "Help:IPA/Malayalam")) is a [city](/wiki/City "City") in the [Kottayam district](/wiki/Kottayam_district "Kottayam district") of [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). It is the district headquarters of the district and is located about 151 km (93.8 mi) north of the state capital [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram"). As per the [2011 Indian census](/wiki/2011_Indian_census "2011 Indian census"), Kottayam has a population of 136,812 people, and a population density of 2,470/km2 (6,400/sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-3)
Kottayam is also referred to as "the City of Letters" [[4]](#cite_note-4) as many of the first [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") daily newspapers, such as *[Deepika](/wiki/Deepika_(newspaper) "Deepika (newspaper)"),* *[Malayala Manorama](/wiki/Malayala_Manorama "Malayala Manorama"),* and *[Mangalam](/wiki/Mangalam_Publications "Mangalam Publications"),* were started and are headquartered in Kottayam, as are a number of publishing houses.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The royal palace of the [Thekkumkur](/wiki/Thekkumkur "Thekkumkur") ruler was protected by a fort called *Thaliyilkotta*. It is believed that the name *Kottayam* is derived from a combination of the [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") words *kotta* which means fort (*Thaliyilkotta*) and *akam* which means inside.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The combined form, *Kottaykkakam* (കോട്ടയ്ക്കകം), can be translated as "inside the fort".[[6]](#cite_note-About_Kottayam-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Kurien-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

### Thekkumkur rule (1103 - 1753)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Thekkumkur rule (1103 - 1753)")]

Main article: [Thekkumkur](/wiki/Thekkumkur "Thekkumkur")

From the beginning of the ninth century AD, the history of [Thekkumkur](/wiki/Thekkumkur "Thekkumkur") and of Kottayam are virtually indistinguishable. Kottayam was then a part of Vempolinad, an area in the [Kulashekara Empire](/wiki/Venad "Venad") (800 AD – 1103  AD). By 1103, the Kingdom of Vempolinad had split into the Kingdoms of Thekkumkur and [Vadakkumkur](/wiki/Vadakkumkur "Vadakkumkur"), and the latter became a vassal of [Cochin](/wiki/Kochi "Kochi").[[9]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The royal house had originally been situated in Vennimala in Kottayam. It was protected by a fort known as Thaliyilkotta and, as a result, the locality came to be known by the same name as the fort. Afterward, Thekkumkur kings shifted their capital to Nattassery near Kumaranallore at the outskirts of Kottayam town. It is believed that the Thekkumkur dynasty ruled Kottayam from Thazhathangadi.

The [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_India "Portuguese India") and the [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_India "Dutch India") established trade relations with both of these kingdoms, dealing in black pepper and other spices. After the subjugation of the Dutch East India Company by the Kingdom of [Travancore](/wiki/Travancore "Travancore") in the 1741 [Travancore–Dutch War](/wiki/Travancore%E2%80%93Dutch_War "Travancore–Dutch War"), military operations of [Marthanda Varma](/wiki/Marthanda_Varma "Marthanda Varma") progressed against the northern neighboring kingdoms, including Thekkumkur.

### Travancore rule (1753 - 1949)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Travancore rule (1753 - 1949)")]

Main article: [Travancore](/wiki/Travancore "Travancore")

Though Thekkumkur allied with Chempakassery and Vadakkumkoor to protect the kingdom, all of them were finally annexed to Travancore.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Another source states that the ruler of Thekkumkur had sided first with the [Kingdom of Kayamkulam](/wiki/Odanad "Odanad") and then with the principality of [Ambalapuzha](/wiki/Ambalapuzha "Ambalapuzha") against Travancore. After the fall of Ambalapuzha, and as the ruler of Thekkumkur refused to come to terms with Travancore, his capital city was taken on 11 September 1750 by [Ramayyan Dalawa](/wiki/Ramayyan_Dalawa "Ramayyan Dalawa"), the general and prime minister of Marthanda Varma, and the state was annexed to Travancore in 1753.

By the early 19th century, Travancore became a Protectorate of Britain after a series of unfair treaties. In 1817, the [Church Missionary Society](/wiki/Church_Missionary_Society "Church Missionary Society") established [CMS College](/wiki/CMS_College_Kottayam "CMS College Kottayam") as the first Western-style college in Kerala. It was welcomed by the [Travancore](/wiki/Travancore "Travancore") government to provide administrators for the public bureaucracy.[[12]](#cite_note-cms-12)

### Contemporary period (1949 - Present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Contemporary period (1949 - Present)")]

Kottayam became a revenue division of Travancore.[[7]](#cite_note-Kurien-7) A fifth division, [Devikulam](/wiki/Devikulam "Devikulam"), existed for a short period but was later added to Kottayam. Kottayam district established in July 1949.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3c/Purple_Swamphen_%28Porphyrio_poliocephalus%29_22-Mar-2007_10-12-15_AM.jpg/250px-Purple_Swamphen_%28Porphyrio_poliocephalus%29_22-Mar-2007_10-12-15_AM.jpg)](/wiki/File:Purple_Swamphen_(Porphyrio_poliocephalus)_22-Mar-2007_10-12-15_AM.jpg)

Purple Swamphen (*[Porphyrio porphyrio](/wiki/Porphyrio_porphyrio "Porphyrio porphyrio")*) photographed in Vembanad Lake, Kottayam

Kottayam has an average elevation of 3 metres (9.8 ft) above sea level.[[14]](#cite_note-14) It is situated in the [basin](/wiki/Drainage_basin "Drainage basin") of the [Meenachil River](/wiki/Meenachil_River "Meenachil River") and in the basin of the [Vembanad](/wiki/Vembanad "Vembanad") backwaters, which are formed from several streams in the [Western Ghats](/wiki/Western_Ghats "Western Ghats") of the [Idukki district](/wiki/Idukki_district "Idukki district"). The town is situated near the inland [estuary](/wiki/Estuary "Estuary") of [Meenachil](/wiki/Meenachil_River "Meenachil River") river where it empties into the [Vembanad](/wiki/Vembanad "Vembanad") lake in [Kumarakom](/wiki/Kumarakom "Kumarakom").

According to the division of places in Kerala based on altitudes, Kottayam is classified as a midland area. The general soil type is [alluvial](/wiki/Alluvium "Alluvium") soil. The vegetation is mainly tropical evergreen and moist deciduous. The district features a mix of hills, back waters and paddy fields.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

The area under Kottayam Municiplaity is 55.40 square kilometers. Kottayam town, the headquarters of Kottayam district is situated in the central part of Kerala, in the Central Travancore region. It is situated in between 9°29' 46" N and 9º 37' 59" North latitude and 76°29' 22" E and 76°34' 24" East longitudes.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Climate")]

Under the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"), Kottayam has a [Tropical monsoon climate](/wiki/Tropical_monsoon_climate "Tropical monsoon climate") (Am).

The climate in this district is moderate and pleasant. Kottayam's proximity to the [equator](/wiki/Equator "Equator") results in little seasonal temperature variation, with moderate to high levels of [humidity](/wiki/Humidity "Humidity").

Kottayam district is bordered by [Pathanamthitta district](/wiki/Pathanamthitta_district "Pathanamthitta district") on the south, [Alappuzha district](/wiki/Alappuzha_district "Alappuzha district") on the west, [Ernakulam district](/wiki/Ernakulam_district "Ernakulam district") on the north and [Idukki district](/wiki/Idukki_district "Idukki district") on the east and northeast.

## Literature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Literature")]

*Jnananikshepam* was the first newspaper published by the natives of Kerala, published at CMS press at Kottayam in 1848.[[24]](#cite_note-24) Kottayam has produced many well-known writers, journalists and artists. Novelist [Muttathu Varkey](/wiki/Muttathu_Varkey "Muttathu Varkey") and poet [Pala Narayanan Nair](/wiki/Pala_Narayanan_Nair "Pala Narayanan Nair") both have roots in Kottayam. [Kottayam Pushpanath](/wiki/Kottayam_Pushpanath "Kottayam Pushpanath"), a writer of crime thrillers lived in Kottayam. He died due to age related ailments on 2 May 2018.[[25]](#cite_note-25) The Indian-English novelist [Arundhati Roy](/wiki/Arundhati_Roy "Arundhati Roy") is a native of Kottayam[[26]](#cite_note-26) and her semi-autobiographical Booker Prize-winning novel, *[The God of Small Things](/wiki/The_God_of_Small_Things "The God of Small Things"),* contains her childhood experiences in [Aymanam](/wiki/Aymanam "Aymanam"), Kottayam. [Unni R.](/wiki/Unni_R. "Unni R.") a story writer and scriptwriter, is also from Kottayam. Kottayam was the first town in India to have achieved 100% literacy.[[27]](#cite_note-27) Kottayam Public Library was founded in 1882 by T Rama RaoDewanPeshkar (Collector) of Northern Division of Travancore State.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Education")]

In the 17th century, a Dutch school was started at Kottayam, which was short-lived. The first English school in Kerala, and the first college in India, was established in 1817 by the [Church Missionary Society](/wiki/Church_Missionary_Society "Church Missionary Society") of England under the leadership of [Col. John Munro](/wiki/John_Munro,_9th_of_Teaninich "John Munro, 9th of Teaninich"), as [CMS College](/wiki/CMS_College_Kottayam "CMS College Kottayam").[[12]](#cite_note-cms-12) Today CMS College Kottayam and [SB College](/wiki/St._Berchmans_College "St. Berchmans College"), established with help of Catholic Church, in [Changanassery](/wiki/Changanassery "Changanassery"), 17 km from town, stands as a landmark and has made a huge contribution to education in the district. Thus, Kottayam became India's first town with 100% literacy in 1989.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

The [Government Medical College, Kottayam](/wiki/Government_Medical_College,_Kottayam "Government Medical College, Kottayam"), is one of the most prominent medical colleges in Kerala. Also, [Mahatma Gandhi University, Kerala](/wiki/Mahatma_Gandhi_University,_Kerala "Mahatma Gandhi University, Kerala"), is based out of Kottayam. Kottayam boasts several other colleges and universities. It is also home to one of the world's most renowned institutes for Syriac studies: [St. Ephrem Ecumenical Research Institute](/wiki/St._Ephrem_Ecumenical_Research_Institute "St. Ephrem Ecumenical Research Institute").[[30]](#cite_note-30)

There are 14 engineering colleges. Government Engineering College - [Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Technology, Kottayam](/wiki/Rajiv_Gandhi_Institute_of_Technology,_Kottayam "Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Technology, Kottayam") is located in Pampady, 14 km east of Kottayam. Indian institute of information technology, Kottayam, an institute of national importance is also located around 30 km (19 mi) from Kottayam, near to Pala town. [Indian Institute of Mass Communication](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Mass_Communication "Indian Institute of Mass Communication") (IIMC) has one of its regional centres near Pampady, Kottayam.

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Media")]

In 1821, [Benjamin Bailey](/wiki/Benjamin_Bailey_(missionary) "Benjamin Bailey (missionary)"), a British missionary, established C.M.S. Press, the first printing press in Kerala, in Kottayam. The town has been at the forefront of newspaper and book publishing in the state ever since.

Newspaper *[Malayala Manorama](/wiki/Malayala_Manorama "Malayala Manorama"),* published from Kottayam, is one of the largest circulating dailies in India.[[31]](#cite_note-31) The Malayala Manorama Group, based in Kottayam, also owns Manorama Online, Manorama News Channel, *[The Week](/wiki/The_Week_(Indian_magazine) "The Week (Indian magazine)")* magazine and other publications. Other major Malayalam newspapers—*[Mathrubhoomi](/wiki/Mathrubhoomi "Mathrubhoomi"),* *[Deshabhimani](/wiki/Deshabhimani "Deshabhimani"),* *[Deepika](/wiki/Deepika_(newspaper) "Deepika (newspaper)"),* *[Madhyamam](/wiki/Madhyamam "Madhyamam"),* and around thirty periodicals are published from Kottayam. Kottayam is also home to several Malayalam book publishers such as [D. C. Books](/wiki/D._C._Books "D. C. Books"), [Labour India Publications](/wiki/Labour_India_Publications "Labour India Publications") and Current Books. Almost 70 percent of books published in Kerala are from Kottayam.[[32]](#cite_note-32) In 1945, a group of writers set up [Sahithya Pravarthaka Sahakarana Sangham](/wiki/Sahithya_Pravarthaka_Co-operative_Society "Sahithya Pravarthaka Co-operative Society") (English: Literary Workers' Co-operative Society) in Malayalam.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Sports")]

A number of annual [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") tournaments including the Marian Trophy, Girideepam Trophy, Lourdes Trophy and Virginia Memorial Tournament are conducted in Kottayam. The main sports stadiums in Kottayam are [Nehru Stadium](/wiki/Nehru_Stadium,_Kottayam "Nehru Stadium, Kottayam") and [Rajiv Gandhi Indoor Stadium](/wiki/Rajiv_Gandhi_International_Indoor_Stadium,_Kottayam "Rajiv Gandhi International Indoor Stadium, Kottayam"), both located in Nagambadom. Native Ball (നാടൻ പന്തുകളി) is also a well known sport in Kottayam.

### Legislature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Legislature")]

Kottayam is one of the six municipalities in the district, formed after the implementation of the Kerala Municipalities Act in 1994. The members of the municipal council are elected from each of 52 wards every five years, held with the local government elections across the state. The chairperson is the executive authority of the municipality.

Kottayam city is the part of the Kottayam legislative assembly constituency and the [Kottayam Lok Sabha constituency](/wiki/Kottayam_(Lok_Sabha_constituency) "Kottayam (Lok Sabha constituency)"). The legislative assembly election is conducted every four years, last in May 2016.

Kottayam Collectorate

The [collectorate](/wiki/Collectorate "Collectorate") of the [Kottayam District](/wiki/Kottayam_District "Kottayam District") is located in Kottayam city. The present collector is Sri. Chetan Kumar Meena IAS. Shri.S.Sreejith IAS serves as Additional District Magistrate and Deputy Collector (General) of Kottayam District.

Many administrative and district offices of Kottayam including the District Court is situated within the collectorate premises.

### Judiciary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Judiciary")]

Five courts were established during the tenure of [Colonel John Munro](/wiki/John_Munro,_9th_of_Teaninich "John Munro, 9th of Teaninich"), as the Diwan of various states in India. One of these was established in [Vaikom](/wiki/Vaikom "Vaikom"), in the northwest of Kottayam district.

The district court at Kottayam was established in 1910 during the period of [Sree Moolam Thirunal Maharaja of Tranvancore](/wiki/Moolam_Thirunal "Moolam Thirunal"). The court celebrated its centenary in 2010.

The District Headquarters of the judiciary is set up at Kottayam city with the Principal District Court as it Administrative Centre. The justice delivery system consists of eight Munsiff Courts, ten Judicial 1st Class Magistrate Courts, three Sub Courts, one Chief Judicial Magistrate Court, and three Additional District Courts. In addition to these regular courts, two Motor Accidents Claims Tribunals, one special court for Vigilance cases and two Family Courts also function in this district.

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Politics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Thiruvanchoor_Radhakrishnan.jpg/250px-Thiruvanchoor_Radhakrishnan.jpg)](/wiki/File:Thiruvanchoor_Radhakrishnan.jpg)

[Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan](/wiki/Thiruvanchoor_Radhakrishnan "Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan"), The current member of legislative assembly (MLA) from Kottayam

The major political parties active in Kottayam are [Indian National Congress](/wiki/Indian_National_Congress "Indian National Congress") (INC), [Communist Party of India (Marxist)](/wiki/Communist_Party_of_India_(Marxist) "Communist Party of India (Marxist)") (CPI(M)), [Bharatiya Janata Party](/wiki/Bharatiya_Janata_Party "Bharatiya Janata Party") (BJP) and [Kerala Congress](/wiki/Kerala_Congress "Kerala Congress"). Trade union movements are also popular in Kottayam as [Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh](/wiki/Bharatiya_Mazdoor_Sangh "Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh") (BMS, Indian Workers' Union), [Indian National Trade Union Congress](/wiki/Indian_National_Trade_Union_Congress "Indian National Trade Union Congress") (INTUC) and [Centre of Indian Trade Unions](/wiki/Centre_of_Indian_Trade_Unions "Centre of Indian Trade Unions") (CITU) affiliated workers engaged in the labor sector.

The current municipal chairperson is M.P Santhoshkumar and Vice-chairperson is Sheeba Punnen. [United Democratic Front (Kerala)](/wiki/United_Democratic_Front_(Kerala) "United Democratic Front (Kerala)") (UDF[K]) is the ruling coalition of parties, holding a majority in the municipal council.

The current member of legislative assembly (MLA) from Kottayam is [Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan](/wiki/Thiruvanchoor_Radhakrishnan "Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan").[[34]](#cite_note-34) He has been of the member of legislative assembly of Kerala representing Kottayam town constituency since 2011.[[35]](#cite_note-35)
